skill.name: pr-description
skill.description: Creates structured pull request descriptions with summaries, diagrams, goals, change lists, testing evidence, reviewer notes, and follow-up work.
skill.summary: Build reviewer-ready pull request descriptions from code changes and testing context.
skill.author: BfdCampos

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown examples and PR template guidance in SKILL.md. The skill does not contain executable code, network calls, credential access, prompt injection text, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged
low_findings.description: The external command findings are true pattern matches but low-risk in context. SKILL.md shows an optional date command for naming a file and example npm test commands to document validation, with no executable script wrapper or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are fenced or inline Markdown examples, not runnable skill code. The commands are common local development commands and do not include network transfer, shell interpolation, or secret access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are PR description text, headings, template labels, or diagram guidance. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, hashing APIs, encryption settings, or authentication code in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation prose and Markdown structure at these locations. There is no semantic evidence of cryptographic implementation or insecure algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are references to diagrams and data relationships in a pull request template. No evidence found of commands that enumerate users, hosts, processes, filesystems, networks, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss Mermaid diagrams and data relationships for PR documentation. They do not request host discovery, system probing, or collection of sensitive machine information.

content.value_statement: Pull request descriptions often miss context, diagrams, and testing evidence reviewers need. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured template for clear review notes.
actual_capabilities: Structures pull request descriptions with summary, goals, changes, testing, reviewer notes, and future work.
Adds Mermaid data flow and entity relationship diagram sections when they are useful.
Prompts for exact testing commands and validation results reviewers can verify.
Helps document business context, breaking changes, assumptions, and dependencies.
Provides examples for strong summaries and testing descriptions.
limitations: It does not inspect git history or generate a diff by itself.
It depends on user-provided change details and test results.
Diagram accuracy requires review against the actual architecture.
It does not publish pull requests or interact with hosting platforms.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Pull Request
use_cases.description: Turn feature notes, changed files, and validation results into a complete PR description for reviewers.

use_cases.title: Document Architecture Changes
use_cases.description: Add data flow and relationship sections when a change affects services, schemas, or integrations.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Notes
use_cases.description: Use a repeatable structure so pull requests include context, testing evidence, assumptions, and follow-up work.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic PR Description
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short summary of changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a PR description for these changes: [brief change summary]. Include summary, goals, changes, testing, reviewer notes, and future work.

prompt_templates.title: Create a PR Description From Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer has change details and test results ready.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete PR description from these notes: [change notes]. Tests run: [commands and results]. Mention assumptions and reviewer focus areas.

prompt_templates.title: Add Diagrams for a Complex Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Change affects data flow or data relationships.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a PR description for this architecture change: [details]. Include Mermaid data flow and entity relationship diagram sections if applicable.

prompt_templates.title: Refine for Senior Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before a high-impact review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this draft PR description for senior reviewers: [draft]. Make risks, breaking changes, validation evidence, dependencies, and follow-up work clear.

output_examples.input: A backend change replaces session cookies with JWT tokens and includes unit, integration, and lint results.
output_examples.output: A structured PR description with a concise summary, authentication flow diagram, goals, grouped changes, exact test commands, results, reviewer focus areas, and future work.

output_examples.input: A database migration adds order items and changes relationships between orders and products.
output_examples.output: A PR description that highlights schema changes, includes an entity relationship diagram section, lists migration validation, and calls out reviewer attention areas.

output_examples.input: A small bug fix changes validation logic and has manual testing notes.
output_examples.output: A compact PR description with the bug context, changed validation behavior, manual test evidence, assumptions, and any deferred automated tests.

best_practices: Provide changed files, business context, test commands, and validation results before asking for the final description.
Review generated diagrams for accuracy before adding them to a pull request.
Call out breaking changes, assumptions, and reviewer focus areas explicitly.
anti_patterns: Do not claim tests passed unless you have actual command results.
Do not include diagrams that are placeholders without review.
Do not hide deferred work or technical debt in vague notes.

faq.question: Does this skill create the pull request?
faq.answer: No. It helps write the description content, but it does not publish or update pull requests.

faq.question: Can it include testing evidence?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for exact commands and results so reviewers can verify the change.

faq.question: Does it require Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: No. It recommends diagrams when data flow or data relationships need explanation.

faq.question: Can it handle small bug fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same structure, but keep sections concise and focused on reviewer needs.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the change summary, important files, tests run, results, risks, assumptions, and planned follow-up work.

faq.question: Is this safe to use in a codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is documentation guidance, but review any suggested commands before running them locally.
